channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.113
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.113 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Configure Copilot coding agent’s validation tools
item.description: When Copilot coding agent writes code, it automatically runs your project’s tests and linter. It also runs GitHub’s security and quality validation tools, including CodeQL, the GitHub Advisory Database, secret… The post Configure Copilot coding agent’s validation tools appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Authentication Tokens Are Not a Data Contract
item.description: Authentication tokens exist to answer one question: is this caller authorized to do this? They are not intended to be a stable data interface, a schema you can depend on, or an input into application logic. If your application decodes tokens and reads claims from them, this is an important...
item.dc:creator: Angel Wong

item.title: Retirement: Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure
item.description: Effective March 31, 2027, the Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure hosted by Power BI will be retired. After this date, the dashboard will no longer be accessible and technical support will be discontinued. We strongly encourage customers to export their
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] General availability: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
item.description: In mid-March 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Publish SQL database projects directly from Visual Studio Code to streamline database deployment workflows. View and edit SQL table data directly in the MSSQ
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift Managed Identity and Workload Identity
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift now supports managed identities and workload identities as a generally available capability, enabling you to run OpenShift clusters and applications on Azure without long lived service principal credentials. This aligns Azure Red H
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public preview: GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension
item.description: The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now introduces GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer, expanding the visual schema design experience with AI-assisted capabilities. Based on developer feedback and evolving workflows, you can now understan
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Database DevOps in SSMS powered by SQL projects
item.description: Bring the benefits of schema-as-code to your database development workflow. SQL database projects enable you to source control your database schema, perform reliable deployments to any environment, and integrate code quality checks into your development p
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: SQL MCP Server
item.description: Announcing SQL MCP Server, our Model Context Protocol (MCP) connector for production databases. SQL MCP Server is a feature rich component of data API builder (DAB) that gives you a simple, predictable, secure way to bring AI agents into your data workflo
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: GitHub Copilot in SQL Server Management Studio 22
item.description: GitHub Copilot is now generally available in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) 22, bringing AI‑powered assistance directly into the application that you use every day. With GitHub Copilot in SSMS, you can use natural language to develop, explain, fix,
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Pin Clustering in .NET MAUI Maps
item.description: .NET MAUI 11 adds pin clustering to the Map control, automatically grouping nearby pins into cluster markers. Learn how to enable clustering, create separate clustering groups, and handle cluster taps on Android and iOS. The post Pin Clustering in .NET MAUI Maps appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Ortinau

item.title: Securing Production Debugging in Kubernetes
item.description: During production debugging, the fastest route is often broad access such as cluster-admin (a ClusterRole that grants administrator-level access), shared bastions/jump boxes, or long-lived SSH keys. It works in the moment, but it comes with two common problems: auditing becomes difficult, and...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Developer CLI (azd): Run and test AI agents locally with azd
item.description: New azd ai agent run and invoke commands let you start and test AI agents from your terminal—locally or in the cloud. The post Azure Developer CLI (azd): Run and test AI agents locally with azd appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: PuiChee (PC) Chan, Travis Angevine

item.title: v1.15.0-beta1
item.description: 1.15.0-beta1 (March 18, 2026) NEW FEATURES: We now produce builds for Windows ARM64 (#32719) You can set a deprecated attribute on variable and output blocks to indicate that they are deprecated. This will produce warnings when passing in a value for a deprecated variable or when referencing a...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: DiskANN Vector Index Improvements
item.description: Remember when we announced the Public Preview of DiskANN vector indexes back in November and mentioned that once you created the index, your table became read‑only? Yeah… about that… 😅 We shipped early because the demand for Vector search in SQL was overwhelming. We knew the constraints weren’t...
item.dc:creator: Davide Mauri, Pooja Kamath

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Lakeflow Connect Free Tier now available in Azure Databricks
item.description: Azure Databricks now offers a Lakeflow Connect Free Tier that gives each workspace 100 free DBUs per workspace per day for ingestion from SaaS applications and databases. This allows you to bring roughly 100 million records of data per workspace per day i
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Versionless key support for transparent data encryption in Azure SQL Database
item.description: Azure SQL Database now supports versionless keys for transparent data encryption (TDE), giving you a simpler and more reliable way to manage your own encryption keys. You no longer need to reference a specific key version from Azure Key Vault or Managed H
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description: The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now introduces Data API builder in public preview with built-in GitHub Copilot integration, bringing a guided back-end generation experience directly into your development workflow. You can generate REST, GraphQL
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.112
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.112 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: GPT-5.3-Codex long-term support in GitHub Copilot
item.description: To give enterprises the stability they need for internal security and safety reviews, GitHub has established a new commitment with long-term support (LTS) models available for Copilot Business and Copilot… The post GPT-5.3-Codex long-term support in GitHub Copilot appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: When Infrastructure Scales But Understanding Doesn’t
item.description: We all know this, even if we don’t like to admit it: modern infrastructure can scale infinitely, but human understanding doesn’t. We’ve all seen it happen – organizations going from managing dozens of servers to thousands of containers, from deploying weekly to deploying hundreds of times per day,...
item.dc:creator: Diego Casati, Ray Kao

item.title: Observability for AI Systems: Strengthening visibility for proactive risk detection
item.description: As AI systems grow more autonomous, observability becomes essential. Learn how visibility into AI behavior helps detect risk and strengthen secure development. The post Observability for AI Systems: Strengthening visibility for proactive risk detection appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Angela Argentati, Matthew Dressman, Habiba Mohamed and Microsoft AI Security

item.title: MSSQL Extension for VS Code: SQL Notebooks, AI-Powered Schema Design, Data API builder & More
item.description: The MSSQL extension for VS Code v1.41 continues to evolve, delivering features that make SQL development more integrated, more powerful, and more developer-friendly. In this release, we’re introducing the Public Preview of Schema Designer with GitHub Copilot, Data API builder, and SQL Notebooks,...
item.dc:creator: Yo-Lei Chen, Tauseef Siddique, Carlos Robles

item.title: Release Notes - 2026-03-05
item.description: Monitor the release status by regions at AKS-Release-Tracker. Announcements Azure Kubernetes Service support for Flatcar Container Linux for AKS (preview) will be retired on 8 June 2026, transition to a supported alternative by that date. From now to 7 June 2026, you can continue to use Flatcar...
item.dc:creator: kaarthis

item.title: Copilot coding agent works faster with semantic code search
item.description: Copilot coding agent now has access to a semantic code search tool, allowing it to find relevant code based on meaning rather than relying on exact text matches with tools… The post Copilot coding agent works faster with semantic code search appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: From 150 Unread to Zero Stress: Automating Inbox Triage with MCP and GitHub Copilot
item.description: Taming the Noisy Inbox: How I Used MCP to Automate Email and Teams Triage How the Model Context Protocol (MCP) turns your AI coding assistant into a workplace productivity engine — connecting Microsoft 365 data to your terminal workflow. The Problem We All Share If you work in a customer-facing...
item.dc:creator: Desi Villanueva

item.title: Azure Skills Plugin – Let’s Get Started!
item.description: Part 2 of the Azure Skills Plugin series Previously: Announcing the Azure Skills Plugin This post is all about getting you up and running. I won’t go deep on capabilities, architecture or anything like that here – that’s coming in future posts in this series. The goal is to get the plugin...
item.dc:creator: Chris Harris

item.title: Secret scanning in AI coding agents via the GitHub MCP Server
item.description: The GitHub MCP Server can now scan your code changes for exposed secrets before you commit or open a pull request. This helps you prevent credential leaks by detecting secrets… The post Secret scanning in AI coding agents via the GitHub MCP Server appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.20 is now generally available
item.description: GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) 3.20 enhances deployment efficiency, monitoring capabilities, code security, and policy management. Here are a few highlights in the 3.20 release: The improved merge experience on the… The post GitHub Enterprise Server 3.20 is now generally available appeared first...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: PowerToys 0.98 is here: new Keyboard Manager UX, the Command Palette Dock and better CursorWrap!
item.description: We’re back with a fresh PowerToys release – PowerToys 0.98! This month introduces the new Command Palette Dock, along with a refreshed Keyboard Manager experience and many other improvements across the utilities. You can grab the update by checking for updates in PowerToys or by heading to the...
item.dc:creator: Niels Laute

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Default Rule Set 2.2 and updates to ruleset support policy
item.description: We are updating the managed ruleset support policy for Azure Web Application Firewall (WAF) following the general availability of Default Rule Set (DRS) 2.2 on Azure Application Gateway and Azure Front Door. This update provides a clearer, more predictabl
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Announcing general availability (GA) of the next-gen Foundry Agent Service—a redesigned API format and runtime experience designed to help teams build and operate agents that can move from prototype to production with confidence. This GA moment is anchore
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Azure VMware Solution AV36P and AV52 node retirement on June 30, 2029
item.description: Microsoft is announcing the retirement of the AV36P and AV52 Nodes on June 30, 2029. The terms of your current AV36P and AV52 Reserved Instance (RI) are not affected by this announcement. At this time, we recommend that you review your current RI expirati
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description: GPT-5.4 mini, OpenAI’s latest fast version of their agentic coding model GPT-5.4, is now rolling out in GitHub Copilot. In early tests, GPT‑5.4 mini appears to be OpenAI’s highest-performing mini… The post GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available for GitHub Copilot appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Kate Catlin

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now includes organization-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity
item.description: Following our enterprise-level release and our user-level release of CLI telemetry, we’re completing coverage with organization-level support. Organization admins can now view CLI-specific activity and usage totals in their 1-day… The post Copilot usage metrics now includes organization-level...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: RT.Assistant: A Multi-Agent Voice Bot Using .NET and OpenAI
item.description: Guest blog post on building a real time assistant using OpenAI Realtime API using .NET, F#, Microsoft.Extensions.AI and .NET MAUI. The post RT.Assistant: A Multi-Agent Voice Bot Using .NET and OpenAI appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Faisal Waris

item.title: Introducing the Azure Cosmos DB Plugin for Cursor
item.description: We’re excited to announce the Cursor plugin for Azure Cosmos DB bringing AI-powered database expertise, best practices guidance, and live database connectivity directly into your coding workflow. Whether you’re designing a new data model, optimizing queries, or building a RAG application with...
item.dc:creator: Sajeetharan Sinnathurai

item.title: Push protection exemptions for roles, teams, and apps
item.description: Organizations with secret scanning push protection can now designate specific roles, teams, and apps as exempt from push protection enforcement. Exemption status is evaluated at the time of each push.… The post Push protection exemptions for roles, teams, and apps appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Investing in the people shaping open source and securing the future together
item.description: See how GitHub is investing in open source security funding maintainers, partnering with Alpha-Omega, and expanding access to help reduce burden and strengthen software supply chains. The post Investing in the people shaping open source and securing the future together appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Kevin Crosby

item.title: GitHub Advanced Security setup made simple
item.description: GitHub Advanced Security is now easier to manage in organizations. A new guided experience helps you set up and configure Advanced Security, so you can now edit configurations and repository… The post GitHub Advanced Security setup made simple appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Azure DevOps Remote MCP Server (public preview)
item.description: When we released the local Azure DevOps MCP Server, it gave customers a way to connect Azure DevOps data with tools like Visual Studio and Visual Studio Code through GitHub Copilot Chat. The next step was to make this experience easier to get started with and to enable it for services that support...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: Code Quality permissions removed from security manager role
item.description: The security manager role can no longer enable or disable GitHub Code Quality for a repository unless they are also an administrator for that repository. Only repository administrators can enable… The post Code Quality permissions removed from security manager role appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The Invisible Rewrite: Modernizing the Kubernetes Image Promoter
item.description: Every container image you pull from registry.k8s.io got there through kpromo, the Kubernetes image promoter. It copies images from staging registries to production, signs them with cosign, replicates signatures across more than 20 regional mirrors, and generates SLSA provenance attestations. If...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Foundry Agent Service is GA: private networking, Voice Live, and enterprise-grade evaluations
item.description: The next-gen Foundry Agent Service is generally available today with end-to-end private networking, Voice Live integration, expanded MCP authentication, GA evaluations with continuous monitoring, and hosted agent deployments in six new Azure regions. The post Foundry Agent Service is GA: private...
item.dc:creator: Bala Venkataraman, jeffhollan, Nick Brady

item.title: Retirement: Support for Windows Server 2016 Marketplace images on Azure Batch pools will end on January 12, 2027
item.description: Azure Batch periodically retires support for operating systems that reach end-of-life status. Support for Windows Server 2016 concluded on January 11, 2022, with extended support scheduled to end on January 12, 2027. Marketplace images for Windows Server
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Migration of Azure Batch Low-Priority VMs to Spot VMs in early March
item.description: Azure Batch had retired Low-Priority VMs on September 30, 2025 and will begin a system-initiated migration to Spot VMs beginning March 1, 2026. This change aligns Batch with the broader Azure Spot infrastructure and simplifies the platform’s VM offering.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Entra ID-Based Access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP
item.description: Microsoft Entra ID-based access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP is now in public preview. This new capability empowers you to use Microsoft Entra ID identities (including guest users via Entra External Identities) to securely connect to Azure Blob Storage via
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: End of life reminder of NVv3 and NVv4-series Azure virtual machine in Azure Batch pools
item.description: Microsoft Azure will retire support for NVv3 or NVv4-series VMs on September 30th, 2026, including NVv3 Series (NVIDIA Tesla M60 GPU): Standard_NV12s_v3, Standard_NV12hs_v3, Standard_NV24s_v3, Standard_NV24ms_v3, Standard_NV32ms_v3, Standard_NV48s_v3 NVv4
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Flatcar Container Linux for AKS (preview)
item.description: Azure Kubernetes Service support for Flatcar Container Linux for AKS (preview) will be retired on June 8, 2026, transition to a supported alternative by that date. From now to June 7, 2026, you can continue to use Flatcar Container Linux for AKS (preview)
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: New Microsoft Purview innovations for Fabric to safely accelerate your AI transformation
item.description: As organizations adopt AI, security and governance remain core primitives for safe AI transformation and acceleration. The post New Microsoft Purview innovations for Fabric to safely accelerate your AI transformation appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Darren Portillo
